title: helianthemum canum subsp. canescens
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. canescens, also known as Rock Rose, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the western United States and Mexico, belonging to the Rockrose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and boost the immune system. Rock Rose can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
